responsibilities: Design precision tooling, dies, jigs, and fixtures using 3D CAD software to support efficient production processes
Perform tolerance stack-up analyses to ensure tooling designs meet GD&T specifications and assembly requirements
Collaborate with manufacturing engineers to optimize tool designs for manufacturability, durability, and cost-efficiency
Conduct mold flow simulations to predict and resolve injection molding defects before tool fabrication
Create detailed engineering drawings, BOMs, and specifications for tooling fabrication by internal or vendor machine shops
Lead tool tryout and validation activities, resolving design issues identified during first article inspection
Evaluate existing tooling for wear, performance, and redesign opportunities to extend tool life and reduce scrap
Coordinate with cross-functional teams to integrate new tooling into production lines within project timelines and budgets
